solution_1: Check MCB/fuse and main power supply. Test with multimeter.
solution_2: Replace start/run capacitor with same rating.
solution_3: Contact authorized service center for motor rewind/replacement.

problem: Motor running but no water discharge
solution_1: Remove priming plug and fill casing completely with water. Prime until water overflows.
solution_2: Check all suction connections with soap water. Tighten or replace gaskets.
solution_3: Open pump casing and check impeller. Tighten on shaft or replace if damaged.

solution_1: Remove and clean foot valve. Clear strainer of debris.
solution_2: Open casing and inspect impeller. Replace if vanes worn down.
solution_3: Seal all suction joints. Check foot valve seal.

solution_1: Tighten all foundation bolts. Check base level.
solution_2: Check shaft runout. Replace shaft if bent beyond tolerance.
solution_3: Open casing and inspect impeller. Remove any obstructions.

solution_1: Allow motor to cool. Install timer for duty cycle control.
solution_2: Check supply voltage. Install voltage stabilizer if fluctuating.
solution_3: Check bearing condition. Replace if rough or noisy.

problem: Pump runs but trips on overload
solution_1: Check current draw vs nameplate. Reduce load or check for blockage.
solution_2: Adjust or replace overload relay to match motor rating.
solution_3: Check all three phases. Repair supply or connections.

solution_1: Replace mechanical seal kit. Check shaft condition.
solution_2: Open pump and replace body gasket. Ensure mating surfaces clean.
solution_3: Inspect casing for cracks. Replace pump body if cracked.

solution_1: Check NPSH. Lower pump or reduce suction lift. Check foot valve.
solution_2: Check bearing play and noise. Replace bearings if worn.
solution_3: Prime pump properly. Fix all air leaks in suction.

solution_1: Replace start capacitor with correct rating.
solution_2: Clean or replace centrifugal switch in motor.
solution_3: Check winding continuity. Service center for repair.

solution_1: Swap any two phase connections to reverse rotation.
solution_2: Check voltage on all three phases. Should be within 2%.

cause_1: Float switch stuck in ON position
solution_1: Clean float switch. Check for debris preventing movement.
solution_2: Test and replace level controller or float switch.

solution_1: Replace discharge check valve.
solution_2: Clean or replace foot valve.

solution_1: Install proper inlet screen. Educate users on disposal.
solution_2: Upgrade to larger pump with better solids handling.
solution_3: Replace impeller and check wear rings.

solution_1: Ensure mechanical seal has water. Add seal flush if needed.
solution_2: Replace mechanical seal. Clean area thoroughly.
solution_3: Clear vent pipe. Ensure proper ventilation.

solution_1: Lower pump position or reduce suction height below 7m.
solution_2: Find and seal all air entry points in suction.

solution_1: Grease bearings properly. Do not over-grease.
solution_2: Replace bearings with correct type and size.

solution_1: Bleed air from pump and system. Check for suction leaks.
solution_2: Inspect and replace worn stage impellers or diffusers.
solution_3: Check electrical supply. Consider VFD installation.

solution_1: Inspect and replace balance piston.
solution_2: Replace thrust bearing assembly.

solution_1: Open discharge valve gradually. Check system for blockage.
solution_2: Check shaft rotation freely. Inspect bearings and seals.
solution_3: Check supply voltage. Should be within nameplate range.

solution_1: Measure impeller clearances. Replace if beyond tolerance.
solution_2: Dismantle pump. Clean all passages and impeller.
solution_3: Prime pump properly. Install air release valves.
